HEADLINE: West Indies becomes first team to progress to Super Eights in T20 World Cup after emphatic win over Nepal
SOURCE: www.hindustantimes.com
PUBLISHED: 2026-02-15 00:00:00 | DOWNLOADED: 2026-02-15 00:00:00
URL: https://www.hindustantimes.com/cricket/west-indies-becomes-first-team-to-progress-to-super-eights-in-t20-world-cup-after-emphatic-win-over-nepal-101771151780052.html

West Indies becomes first team to progress to Super Eights in T20 World Cup after emphatic win over Nepal

"I thought our bowlers bowled a really tight line."
The result eliminates Nepal after three defeats. Their final group match is against Scotland in Mumbai on Tuesday. The West Indies' final group game will be against Italy in Kolkata on Thursday. The Caribbean side will then return to Mumbai for their first Super Eights match on February 23, with their opponents still to be decided.

═══ [2] ═══════════════════════════════════════
HEADLINE: What if rain washes out India vs Pakistan T20 World Cup match in Colombo? ICC reserve day and overs rule decoded
SOURCE: www.hindustantimes.com
PUBLISHED: 2026-02-15 00:00:00 | DOWNLOADED: 2026-02-15 00:00:00
URL: https://www.hindustantimes.com/cricket/what-if-rain-washes-out-india-vs-pakistan-t20-world-cup-match-in-colombo-icc-reserve-day-and-overs-rule-decoded-101771133707311.html

What if rain washes out India vs Pakistan T20 World Cup match in Colombo? ICC reserve day and overs rule decoded

Both teams head into the contest on the back of two wins. India survived a tricky opener against the USA before the defending champions cruised past Namibia in Delhi. Pakistan, the 2009 champions, were pushed hard by the Netherlands in their first game before overcoming the USA to build momentum. For the fifth consecutive T20 World Cup edition, India and Pakistan are set to meet in the group stage. Historically, India have dominated this rivalry at the tournament, winning seven of their eight encounters. ALSO READ: Rivalry or not, this is a match India and Pakistan are desperate to not lose
While India will look to extend that supremacy, weather models indicate a high probability of rain in Colombo during the day. Forecasts indicate conditions may ease in the evening, but interruptions remain a strong possibility. The Group A fixture is scheduled to begin at 7:00 PM (IST). In the event of delays, the umpires and match referee must ensure that any revised contest concludes by 11:10 PM, the official cut-off time, inclusive of an additional 60-minute buffer. As per the ICC’s Playing Conditions for the 2026 T20 World Cup, a minimum of five overs per side is required for a result in group-stage matches. “Save for circumstances where a match is awarded to a team as a consequence of the opposing team’s refusal to play (clause 16.2), all matches in which both teams have not had an opportunity of batting for a minimum of five overs shall be declared a No Result,” the regulations state. (The requirement increases to 10 overs per side in semi-finals and the final.)
Importantly, there is no reserve day for group-stage fixtures. If rain washes out the India-Pakistan match in Colombo, both teams will share one point each. Beyond the sporting stakes, a washout would carry significant commercial implications. Broadcasters would risk a dip in advertising revenue, while Sri Lanka Cricket could be compelled to process ticket refunds in the event of an abandonment.

═══ [3] ═══════════════════════════════════════
HEADLINE: West Indies beat Nepal, become first team into T20 World Cup Super Eights
SOURCE: www.aljazeera.com
PUBLISHED: 2026-02-15 00:00:00 | DOWNLOADED: 2026-02-15 00:00:00
URL: https://www.aljazeera.com/sports/2026/2/15/west-indies-beat-nepal-become-first-team-into-t20-world-cup-super-eights?traffic_source=rss

West Indies beat Nepal, become first team into T20 World Cup Super Eights

The West Indies have become the first team to qualify for the Super Eights phase of the T20 World Cup when they cruised to victory against Nepal by nine wickets in India. After restricting Nepal to 133-8 in their 20 overs on Sunday, captain Shai Hope’s 61 not out helped his team romp to 134-1 in 15.2 overs in the Group C clash at Mumbai’s Wankhede Stadium. Recommended Stories
list of 3 items
list 1 of 3‘Heartbreak on Feb 15’: Pakistan fans brace for T20 World Cup loss to India
list 2 of 3Why India vs Pakistan is a godsend for T20 World Cup hosts Sri Lanka
list 3 of 3Real Madrid thrash Real Sociedad 4-1 to move top of La Liga
end of list
Hope reached his fifty off 44 balls and scored three sixes and five fours in all. He put on an unbroken 91 with Shimron Hetmyer to reach the 134 target with 28 balls to spare. Hetmyer was 46 not out at the end from 32 balls with two sixes and four fours. After Hope won the toss and chose to field, Matthew Forde bowled a magnificent spell of 1-10 from his four overs and removed Nepal captain Rohit Paudel for five. Nepal were in all sorts of trouble at 22-3 at the end of the six-over powerplay, then 23-4 and 46-5 in the 11th over as Jason Holder also got among the wickets. It was only Dipendra Singh Airee’s 58 that pushed Nepal past the 100 mark as he shared in a partnership of 54 for the seventh wicket with Sompal Kami, who was there at the end on 26 not out. Holder took 4-27 from his four overs. The result eliminates Nepal, who cannot progress further in this year’s T20 World Cup.
